data.title: Berkshire Hathaway Shakes Up Portfolio: What’s Behind Buffett’s Surprising Moves?
data.description:
Warren Buffett’s Berkshire Hathaway (BRK.A) (BRK.B) has made significant moves recently in its portfolio, reflecting a dynamic strategy in response to market conditions. In the latest transactions, Berkshire increased its stake in several companies while reducing its holdings in others. The question arises: What does Berkshire see that others don’t, and is it signaling a shift in the hedge fund’s investing strategy? First, let’s examine the recent movements and then deduce their meaning.

Maximize Your Portfolio with Data Driven Insights:

  • Leverage the power of TipRanks' Smart Score, a data-driven tool to help you uncover top performing stocks and make informed investment decisions.
  • Monitor your stock picks and compare them to top Wall Street Analysts' recommendations with Your Smart Portfolio

Increased Stakes, Reduced Stakes

In recent strategic moves, Berkshire Hathaway significantly boosted its stake in Occidental Petroleum (OXY) to over 28% by purchasing an additional 763,000 shares on February 7, 2025, bringing its total investment to $12.9 billion. Additionally, Berkshire acquired in the fourth quarter of 2024, 5.6 million shares of Constellation Brands (STZ), a beer and wine producer valued at over $1 billion. Berkshire also nearly doubled its holdings in Domino’s Pizza (DPZ) to 2.4 million shares during the same period. The company also increased its stake in Pool Corporation (POOL), a seller of swimming pool supplies. It boosted its stake in Heico Corporation (HEI), an industrial components maker, which saw a 51% rise in stock value in 2024.

Conversely, Berkshire Hathaway reduced its positions in several other companies. In the fourth quarter of 2024, the conglomerate reduced its stake in Bank of America (BAC) by 15% to 680 million shares and decreased its holdings in Citigroup (C) by approximately 75% to 14.6 million. Berkshire also trimmed its stake in Apple (AAPL) by 25%, selling 100 million shares during the same period. Additionally, the company sold its shares in Ulta Beauty (ULTA), leading to a 0.9% drop in Ulta’s stock post-sale.

Does it Reflect a Shift in Strategy?

Berkshire’s recent moves reflect a strategic pivot towards sectors with perceived growth potential and stability. Increasing stakes in companies like Occidental Petroleum and Constellation Brands suggest focusing on industries with robust demand and resilient business models. The investment in Domino’s Pizza highlights confidence in the fast-food sector’s continued growth, driven by strong consumer demand and new delivery models.

Conversely, reducing stakes in Bank of America and Citigroup indicates a cautious approach toward the banking sector, possibly due to regulatory uncertainties and market volatility. Despite its strong performance, trimming the Apple stake might be a move to lock in profits and reallocate capital to other opportunities.

These strategic shifts highlight Berkshire’s adaptive investment philosophy, balancing risk and reward while steering in an ever-changing market landscape. Investors will keenly watch Berkshire’s next moves as the conglomerate continues to shape its portfolio for future growth. But does this shift reflect a change in Berkshire’s investing strategy, moving away from the banking and tech industries and solidifying positions in the energy, industrial, and food industries?

The commonality between all these companies lies in their robust demand and resilient business models, which align with Berkshire’s focus on long-term stability and growth potential. So, there is no shift, just a slight rearrangement.

Is Berkshire Hathaway a Good Stock to Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, Berkshire Hathaway is considered a Moderate Buy. The average price target for BRK.A stock is $803.444, implying an 11.72% upside potential. The average price target for BRK.B is $536, indicating an 11.76% upside potential.

See more BRK.A BRK.B analyst ratings
data.author: Ran Melamed

data.title: Broadcom and TSMC in Talks to Split Intel Into Two Distinct Entities
data.description:
According to the Wall Street Journal, Intel (INTC), once the king of chip-making, is now on the brink of a major split. Industry heavyweights Broadcom (AVGO) and Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSMC) are potentially in talks to divide the American tech giant into two distinct entities. The once undisputed chip-making king looks set to endure its own ‘Partitions of Poland’ ordeal. Almost all the major players in the chip industry have been circling Intel’s floating body and trying to stake their claim over the territory. Just a few months ago, Qualcomm (QCOM) entertained the idea of acquiring Intel, so it seems that Intel has entered a determinist path in which selling parts or its entire assets is becoming increasingly likely.

Maximize Your Portfolio with Data Driven Insights:

  • Leverage the power of TipRanks' Smart Score, a data-driven tool to help you uncover top performing stocks and make informed investment decisions.
  • Monitor your stock picks and compare them to top Wall Street Analysts' recommendations with Your Smart Portfolio

Intel’s Potential Breakup

Insiders say Broadcom is interested in Intel’s chip design and marketing division. However, it will likely only move forward if they find a partner to take over Intel’s manufacturing arm. On the other hand, TSMC is considering taking control of some or all of Intel’s factories, possibly teaming up with investors to make it happen. These discussions are still in the early stages, and no formal offers have been made yet.

The idea of breaking up Intel might have seemed outlandish until recently. However, the company’s recent struggles, such as falling behind TSMC in advanced chip production and the ousting of CEO Pat Gelsinger in December, have left it vulnerable. Splitting the company could align Intel with a growing industry trend where companies focus on designing or manufacturing chips, but not both.

National Security and Operational Hurdles

Frank Yeary, Intel’s interim executive chairman, has led talks with potential buyers and U.S. government officials, who closely monitor the situation due to national security concerns. Any deal involving TSMC taking control of Intel factories would need U.S. government approval, especially given Intel’s significant funding from the 2022 Chips Act.

Intel has already taken steps to separate its manufacturing unit, operate it independently, and prepare it for outside investment. However, significant challenges remain, such as retooling Intel’s factories to meet TSMC’s high standards and navigating immigration restrictions that could limit TSMC’s ability to bring in engineers from abroad.

While no final decisions have been made, Intel’s potential breakup marks a dramatic shift for a company that once dominated the chip-making landscape. As Broadcom and TSMC consider their next moves, Intel’s future hangs in the balance, signaling a new era in the global semiconductor industry.

Is Intel a Buy, Hold, or a Sell?

Turning to Wall Street, Intel is considered a Hold with an average price target of $22.40. This implies a 5.08% downside potential.

See more INTC analyst ratings
data.author: Ran Melamed

data.title: Is the Stock Market Open Tomorrow, President’s Day?
data.description:
The U.S. stock market will be closed tomorrow, February 17, 2025, in honor of President’s Day.

Maximize Your Portfolio with Data Driven Insights:

  • Leverage the power of TipRanks' Smart Score, a data-driven tool to help you uncover top performing stocks and make informed investment decisions.
  • Monitor your stock picks and compare them to top Wall Street Analysts' recommendations with Your Smart Portfolio

Keep track of all the stock market holidays with TipRanks’ Stock Market Holidays calendar.

Will the Holiday Effect Impact the Markets This Week?

Understanding how the stock market might be affected by the holiday will help you invest well and beat the markets. Investors sometimes refer to the pre-holiday market rise and the post-holiday price drift as the “holiday effect.”

The holiday effect is linked to investors’ general optimism and confidence. The fact that customers tend to spend more money around the holidays is one explanation for the pre-holiday market surge. The share prices of retailers, in particular, may increase as a result.

As for the post-holiday price drift, one reason is that many traders and market analysts take time off work during the holidays to relax from their regular trading and investment research schedules. Those investors view the holidays as a time to unwind and refresh. Alternately, some traders see the season as a chance to work harder and get some tactical advantages in the market, maybe by taking advantage of the period when many other traders take time off.

Watch this space to learn about the President’s Day holiday effect on the stock market. And remember to always use TipRanks to stay up-to-date on your stock research.
data.author: TipRanks Team

data.title: Tesla and Lucid: This Analyst Selects His Top EV Stocks to Buy as Growth Is Set to Accelerate
data.description:
Last year saw a slowdown in the market for electric vehicles, along with some increased concern that EVs are simply not going to replace the internal combustion engine. Major auto companies, such as Ford Motor, are losing money on every EV they produce, and Akio Toyoda, chairman of Toyota, has publicly said that battery electric vehicles are not the future of the auto industry.

Maximize Your Portfolio with Data Driven Insights:

  • Leverage the power of TipRanks' Smart Score, a data-driven tool to help you uncover top performing stocks and make informed investment decisions.
  • Monitor your stock picks and compare them to top Wall Street Analysts' recommendations with Your Smart Portfolio

Yet, the road ahead isn’t all gloom. Plenty of bullish voices still back the EV sector. Benchmark analyst Mickey Legg, for one, sees strong tailwinds that could power the market forward.

“The number of EVs on US roads is expected to reach 78.5M in 2035, up from 4.5M in 2023, a CAGR of 26.9% (Source: EEI). By 2050, 89% of all US vehicles are expected to be BEV or plug-in hybrid, vs 8% this year (Source: BloombergNEF). While we believe these estimates are somewhat aspirational, the industry will expand rapidly once domestic manufacturing and charging infrastructure are sufficiently built out, reducing sticker prices to the mass market level and alleviating charging anxiety,” Legg stated.

Given this background, Legg goes on to tag Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) and Lucid (NASDAQ:LCID) as the top EV stocks to buy for investors looking to capitalize on an acceleration of the EV market. But do other analysts share this optimism? We turned to the TipRanks database to find out.

Tesla

Leading the charge is Tesla, the biggest and most recognizable name in the EV world. Its dominance isn’t just about the cars – it’s also fueled by the relentless promotion and vision of its CEO, Elon Musk. Whether seen as a genius or a showman, Musk has steered Tesla to a milestone no other 21st-century EV startup has reached: sustained profitability. Under his leadership, Tesla hasn’t just grown – it has reshaped the auto industry, becoming the world’s largest automaker by market value. With a market cap of $1.14 trillion, the company is 4.5 times more valuable than second-place Toyota.

Tesla has been turning profits since 2020, and the bulk of those profits come from its lineup of marketable electric vehicles. The company has focused on pure-play battery-powered vehicles (BEVs), and Musk is justly proud of the firm’s innovations in battery technology that have improved both battery lifespan and vehicle performance. Tesla’s most popular cars are the Model 3 and the Model Y, which, by a wide margin, account for the lion’s share of the company’s production, deliveries, and revenues.

The popularity of these two models is evident in Tesla’s production numbers. Early last month, the company released its production and delivery figures for 4Q24, reporting totals of 459,445 cars built and 495,570 delivered. Of these, Models 3 and Y accounted for 436,718 in production and 471,930 in deliveries. The company also deployed 11 GWh of energy storage products and reported that both vehicle deliveries and energy storage deployments reached quarterly record levels.

Turning to financial results, Tesla posted total revenues of $25.7 billion in 4Q24, reflecting a 2% year-over-year increase, though this fell $1.44 billion short of expectations. The company’s automotive revenue, at $19.8 billion, declined 8% y/y, while its energy generation and storage revenue surged 113% to $3.06 billion. At the bottom line, Tesla posted a non-GAAP profit of 73 cents per share, falling 4 cents short of expectations.

While EVs remain Tesla’s core business, the company isn’t betting its future on just one lane. With AI shaping the next tech revolution, Tesla is accelerating its efforts to weave artificial intelligence into everyday machines. From autonomous vehicles to advanced robotics, the company is driving innovation with a bold vision for the future.

The company is developing Full Self-Driving (FSD) software aimed at making fully autonomous vehicles road-safe and reliable. In line with this, it has recently unveiled its Cybercab robotaxi. Tesla is also working in the field of robotics to design and build an autonomous humanoid robot. The robot, dubbed Optimus, was made public several years ago. It’s designed to operate in the human world – it is 5-foot-8, and its arms and legs are built to human proportions – making it suitable for carrying out a wide range of general-purpose tasks. This combination of AI tech and robotic movement is in its early stages, and Optimus still relies on teleoperation for complex actions. Despite the limitations, Musk believes that autonomy and robotics are the future of Tesla.

That vision has caught the attention of Benchmark’s Mark Legg, who likes Tesla as a long-term investment.

“Tesla has multiple opportunities to fuel growth including autonomous vehicles, robotics, and energy generation/storage, as well as continued proliferation of the EV market. Advancements in manufacturing and battery technology have driven down market prices and we see the release of more affordable Tesla models in 2025 as a key catalyst for the stock. The company has outlined a path for growth with a more affordable vehicle scheduled for 1H25, unsupervised full self-driving as a paid service this June in Austin, TX, and Optimus robot production ramp through 2026 and beyond. Our model has only baked in vehicle growth, providing significant potential upside should autonomous vehicles and Optimus achieve scale,” Legg noted.

Quantifying his stance, the analyst puts a Buy rating on TSLA shares, along with a $475 price target that implies a one-year gain of 33% for the stock. (To watch Legg’s track record, click here)

This is the bullish argument. Overall, Tesla has a Hold (i.e., Neutral) consensus rating from the Street, based on 35 recent analyst reviews that include 13 Buys, 12 Holds, and 10 Sells. The shares are trading for $355.84, while their $340.50 average price target suggests they will remain rangebound for the time being. (See TSLA stock forecast)

Lucid Group

The second EV stock we’re looking at, Lucid Group, designs and manufactures high-end luxury EVs. Based in California, the company has developed a vehicle lineup that incorporates the latest EV technologies alongside luxurious styling for both exteriors and interiors.

The company has brought all of this together in two vehicle models, the Air and the Gravity. The Lucid Air is available in a wide range of body styles and interiors, and customers can choose from a set of exterior and interior options to effectively customize their vehicle from the factory floor. Lucid’s vehicles feature such attractions as high-visibility glass canopy design, electronic ‘glass’ dashboards, battery ranges estimated at 400-plus miles, and plenty of passenger legroom and trunk space for luggage. The vehicles are designed to make driving comfortable, and the company can also provide a driver assistance package.

The Air is a sedan model. The newly released Gravity is an all-electric SUV boasting two electric motors for an all-wheel drive feature – and enhanced cargo space in the form of a front trunk where other cars keep their gasoline engines. The vehicle is offered with two- or three-row seating, for up to 7 seven passengers. The Gravity was opened for customer orders for the 2025 model year; the Grand Touring version can be ordered now, while the lower-cost Touring version will be available for customer order later this year.

Production began on the Air in 2021, and on the Gravity last year. In Lucid’s last quarterly production and delivery report, covering 4Q24, the company reported total production of 3,386 vehicles along with 3,099 vehicle deliveries. For the full-year 2024, Lucid’s production came to 9,029 vehicles and deliveries reached a total of 10,241 vehicles. Lucid’s full-year production was in-line with the company’s guidance, published with the 3Q24 earnings results.

The 3Q24 earnings report was the last released, and showed revenues of $200 million, up 45% year-over-year and some $2.26 million better than had been anticipated. The company runs a net earnings loss, which in 3Q24 came to 28 cents per share by non-GAAP measures – although it beat the forecast by 3 cents per share. The company had $5.16 billion in total liquidity at the end of the third quarter.

Lucid will release its 4Q24 results later this month. Analysts are expecting to see total revenue of approximately $211 million.

In his coverage of Lucid, Benchmark’s Legg sees several advantages for the company, including its high technological level, its strong cash reserves, and its vehicle line-up.

“After a pause in 2024, domestic EV production is expected to improve in 2025 and further accelerate in 2026-27 as ASPs decline and the charging infrastructure is built out. We view LCID as well positioned to achieve significant share of this burgeoning opportunity based on its advanced technology, well-stocked balance sheet and access to capital, award-winning vehicles, Saudi investment, partnerships and highly integrated manufacturing capabilities… While the Trump administration has taken aim at rolling back incentives for the EV industry, we see this as further extending the window for Lucid to gain market share from more traditional brands as they delay their transition to electric even further,” Legg opined.

To this end, Legg rates LCID shares as a Buy, and he complements that with a $5 price target showing his confidence in a robust one-year upside potential of 74%.

The broader Street view, however, is less enthusiastic. Lucid’s consensus rating is a Hold, based on 7 recent reviews that break down to 1 Buy, 4 Holds, and 2 Sells. The stock is priced at $3.31, and its average price target price of $2.93 suggests an 11% downside from current levels. (See LCID stock forecast)

To find good ideas for EV stocks trading at attractive valuations, visit TipRanks’ Best Stocks to Buy, a tool that unites all of TipRanks’ equity insights.

Disclaimer: The opinions expressed in this article are solely those of the featured analyst. The content is intended to be used for informational purposes only. It is very important to do your own analysis before making any investment.
data.author: Michael Marcus

data.title: OpenAI Rejects Elon Musk’s Takeover Offer
data.description:
OpenAI has categorically rejected Elon Musk’s proposed $97.4 billion takeover offer, saying it was “not a bid at all.”

Maximize Your Portfolio with Data Driven Insights:

  • Leverage the power of TipRanks' Smart Score, a data-driven tool to help you uncover top performing stocks and make informed investment decisions.
  • Monitor your stock picks and compare them to top Wall Street Analysts' recommendations with Your Smart Portfolio

In a letter to Musk’s attorney, OpenAI’s lawyer William Savitt said that OpenAI’s board of directors had reviewed the proposal and determined that it is not in the best interest of OpenAI’s mission and is “rejected.” OpenAI added that the decision by its board is “unanimous.”

In a written statement released to the media, OpenAI Board Chair Bret Taylor said that the company “is not for sale.” The rejection comes days after Musk and a group of investors offered to buy artificial intelligence (AI) startup OpenAI for $97.4 billion. Musk has said he wants to return the startup to its nonprofit roots.

‘No Thank You’

Shortly after Musk’s offer was made, OpenAI CEO Sam Altman wrote on social media platform X, formerly known as Twitter, “No thank you but we will buy Twitter for $9.74 billion if you want.” Musk is the owner of X/Twitter. Musk then referred to Altman in his own post on X as “Scam Altman.”

Musk, and others, claim to be concerned with Altman’s plans to shift OpenAI to a for-profit business model. Musk and Altman co-founded OpenAI but have had a falling out over the direction of the company. Musk is currently suing OpenAI over breach of contract and to try and prevent its transition to a for-profit structure.

Neither OpenAI or X are publicly traded companies.

Is TSLA Stock a Buy?

Given the privately held nature of OpenAI and X, we’ll look instead at the stock of electric vehicle maker Tesla (TSLA), which Elon Musk also owns and that it the main source of his wealth. Tesla stock currently has a consensus Hold rating among 35 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 13 Buy, 12 Hold, and 10 Sell recommendations issued in the last three months. The average TSLA price target of $340.50 implies 4.31% downside risk from current levels.

Read more analyst ratings on TSLA stock
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Constellation Brands (STZ) Stock Jumps on News Warren Buffett Has Taken a Position
data.description:
Shares of Constellation Brands (STZ) are up 7% on news that legendary investor Warren Buffett has opened a new position in the alcoholic drink maker.

Maximize Your Portfolio with Data Driven Insights:

  • Leverage the power of TipRanks' Smart Score, a data-driven tool to help you uncover top performing stocks and make informed investment decisions.
  • Monitor your stock picks and compare them to top Wall Street Analysts' recommendations with Your Smart Portfolio

STZ stock was the only new security purchased by Buffett’s holding company Berkshire Hathaway during the fourth and final quarter of 2024. Rochester, New York-based Constellation Brands makes a number of beer, wine and spirit brands, including Corona, Robert Mondavi, and Casa Noble Tequila.

Berkshire Hathaway purchased 5.62 million STZ shares worth $1.24 billion in the October through December quarter. For Constellation Brands, the investment by Warren Buffett is a big vote of confidence in the company. STZ stock has declined 32% over the last 12 months and is down 14% over the past five years amid declining alcohol consumption, particularly among younger consumers.

Other Buffett Moves

Other moves made by Warren Buffett during Q4 2024 include lowering its stake in U.S. bank Citigroup (C) by 74% to 14.6 million shares, and reducing its Bank of America (BAC) stake, until recently its second-largest holding, by 15% to 680.2 million shares.

Berkshire Hathaway appears to have stopped selling shares of Apple (AAPL), its biggest position, finishing 2024 with 300 million shares of the tech company valued at $73.38 billion. Apple now comprises 24.8% of Buffett’s portfolio, down from 50% at the end of 2023. Berkshire completely exited its position in Ulta Beauty (ULTA) during the period.

BRK.B stock has gained 20% over the last 12 months.

Is BRK.B Stock a Buy?

Berkshire Hathaway’s Class B stock has a consensus Moderate Buy rating among one Wall Street analyst. That rating is based on one Buy recommendation issued in the last three months. The average BRK.B price target of $536 implies 11.76% upside from current levels.

Read more analyst ratings on BRK.B stock
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Elon Musk’s xAI Is Seeking to Raise $10B at $75B Valuation
data.description:
xAI, a generative artificial intelligence startup led by Tesla (TSLA) CEO Elon Musk, is seeking to raise $10 billion in new funding, which would value the company at $75 billion, according to Bloomberg. This funding round would be a significant increase from the company’s previous valuation of $50 billion, which was based on a $6 billion funding round from late last year. The new funding is expected to support xAI’s ongoing development of its AI technology, such as its Grok chatbot, which has already gained significant attention.

Maximize Your Portfolio with Data Driven Insights:

  • Leverage the power of TipRanks' Smart Score, a data-driven tool to help you uncover top performing stocks and make informed investment decisions.
  • Monitor your stock picks and compare them to top Wall Street Analysts' recommendations with Your Smart Portfolio

The new funding round is expected to involve previous investors like Sequoia Capital, Andreessen Horowitz, and Valor Equity Partners. xAI has been actively developing its technology and has made significant investments in its infrastructure, such as a massive data center in Memphis, which will utilize 100,000 of Nvidia’s H100 GPUs. The company also released its first API and has launched its official Grok app for iOS. Additionally, xAI has announced plans to generate $100 million in annual revenue, although most of this revenue is expected to come from Musk’s other companies, including the social network X.

The funding round comes as competition heats up against other major players like Microsoft-backed OpenAI (MSFT). In fact, a Musk-led group recently made an unsolicited offer to buy the non-profit entity that controls OpenAI for $97.4 billion, with xAI reportedly involved in the offer. Some have speculated that the offer may be a strategic move by Musk to slow down OpenAI’s capital-raising efforts. Musk is also currently suing OpenAI in an attempt to stop its for-profit conversion.

Is Tesla Stock a Buy, Hold, or Sell?

Although retail investors cannot invest in xAI or most of Musk’s ventures, they can invest in his most popular company, Tesla. When turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on TSLA stock based on 13 Buys, 12 Holds, and 10 Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 77% rally in its share price over the past year, the average Tesla price target of $340.50 per share implies 4.3% downside risk.

See more TSLA analyst ratings
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: Nvidia (NVDA) Reduces Stake in Chipmaker Arm Holdings
data.description:
Regulatory filings show that Nvidia (NVDA) has reduced its investment in British chipmaker Arm Holdings (ARM) by about 44%.

Maximize Your Portfolio with Data Driven Insights:

  • Leverage the power of TipRanks' Smart Score, a data-driven tool to help you uncover top performing stocks and make informed investment decisions.
  • Monitor your stock picks and compare them to top Wall Street Analysts' recommendations with Your Smart Portfolio

Silicon Valley-based Nvidia cut its stake in Arm by 43.8% to 1.1 million shares, valuing its remaining investment at $181 million. Arm, whose stock is down about 3% on news of the share sale, supplies the intellectual property that firms such as Nvidia and Apple (AAPL) license to create their microchips and processors.

Investors carefully parse Nvidia’s investment moves for insights into the company’s strategy and direction, particularly in the area of artificial intelligence (AI). Nvidia isn’t the only company to sell down its stake in Arm Holdings. Intel (INTC) recently sold off its entire stake in Arm amid that company’s current restructuring.

Other Moves

Beyond Arm Holdings, Nvidia exited its entire stake in SoundHound AI (SOUN) in the fourth quarter of last year, according to filings with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC). Nvidia also dissolved its holding in Israel-based medtech company Nano-X Imaging Ltd.

In terms of new positions, Nvidia purchased 1.7 million shares in China’s self-driving startup WeRide in the final months of 2024. WeRide uses Nvidia’s processors and AI software to power its vehicles. Nvidia’s own stock has risen 91% over the last 12 months.

Is NVDA Stock a Buy?

Nvidia’s stock currently has a consensus Strong Buy rating among 40 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 37 Buy and three Hold recommendations assigned in the last three months. The average NVDA price target of $179.03 implies 29.01% upside from current levels.

Read more analyst ratings on NVDA stock
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Meta Platforms (META) Stock Extends Record Win Streak to 20 Consecutive Days
data.description:
Meta Platforms (META) stock just keeps on winning.

Maximize Your Portfolio with Data Driven Insights:

  • Leverage the power of TipRanks' Smart Score, a data-driven tool to help you uncover top performing stocks and make informed investment decisions.
  • Monitor your stock picks and compare them to top Wall Street Analysts' recommendations with Your Smart Portfolio

Shares of the social media company are on pace to extend their record win to 20 consecutive trading days, propelling the stock to an all-time high in the process. Shares of Meta Platforms are up 1.44% on Feb. 14, having risen for 20 days in a row. The current rally has crushed the previous longest rally in the shares that lasted 11 days back in September 2015.

META stock hasn’t had a down day since Jan. 16, which was the day President-elect Donald Trump was weighing an executive order to delay the U.S. ban on rival social media app TikTok. The rally began in the final days of the previous administration of U.S. President Joe Biden and is longer than any rally that’s occurred in another Magnificent Seven stock.

Defying Expectations

META stock has also continued to steadily rise despite several events and news that has shaken the broader market, including the emergence of Chinese artificial intelligence (AI) start-up DeepSeek, threats of trade tariffs, and hot inflation data. So far this year, Meta’s stock has gained 27%, trouncing the 3% gain in the benchmark S&P 500 index during the same period.

Analysts attribute META stock’s hot streak to the company’s aggressive AI strategy. Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg has said the company will invest up to $65 billion in the technology this year, with a focus on AI infrastructure. At the same time, Meta reported fourth-quarter 2024 financial results that topped Wall Street expectations, giving the stock added momentum.

Is META Stock a Buy?

The stock of Meta Platforms has a consensus Strong Buy rating among 48 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 44 Buy, three Hold, and one Sell recommendations issued in the past three months. The average META price target of $764.61 implies 3.37% upside from current levels.

Read more analyst ratings on META stock
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Dell Is Close to Securing a Massive Contract with Elon Musk’s xAI
data.description:
Shares of Dell Technologies (DELL) are up in today’s trading after Bloomberg reported that the tech hardware company is in advanced discussions to secure a massive contract worth over $5 billion to provide servers optimized for artificial intelligence to Elon Musk’s xAI. The deal would involve Dell selling servers equipped with Nvidia (NVDA) GB200 semiconductors to xAI, with delivery expected this year.

Maximize Your Portfolio with Data Driven Insights:

  • Leverage the power of TipRanks' Smart Score, a data-driven tool to help you uncover top performing stocks and make informed investment decisions.
  • Monitor your stock picks and compare them to top Wall Street Analysts' recommendations with Your Smart Portfolio

The potential contract illustrates the growing demand for high-powered servers capable of running AI workloads. Dell, along with other server manufacturers like Super Micro Computer (SMCI) and Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE), has seen a surge in demand from companies like Tesla (TSLA) and xAI.

As a result, analysts expect Dell’s AI server business to continue booming, with forecasts suggesting the company will ship $14 billion worth of AI servers in the fiscal year ending January 2026. This would be a significant increase from the estimated $10 billion worth of AI servers shipped in the previous fiscal year. The deal with xAI is expected to be a major contributor to this growth, although its impact on profitability is less clear. In addition, according to Bloomberg Intelligence analyst Woo Jin Ho, the deal “would firmly establish the company as a leading AI-server provider and boost sales.”

Is DELL Stock a Good Buy Now?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on DELL stock based on 14 Buys, two Holds, and zero Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 39% rally in its share price over the past year, the average DELL price target of $147.74 per share implies 30% upside potential.
